perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms will have the meanings specified below: “Affiliate” means, with respect to party, any individual, Partner, corporation, partnership or other entity, directly or indirectly, controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such party.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms will have the meanings specified below.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the definition of 'Affiliate' creates an in-text contradiction because Section 4 references 'Affiliates' without a clear definition. This creates ambiguity about the scope of compliance, as the term is used without context.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of ADP and the Partner will be responsible for compliance by its respective Affiliates with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliate'. The changed text removes this definition. The contradicted text states that 'Each of ADP and the Partner will be responsible for compliance by its respective Affiliates with the terms of this Agreement.' Without the definition of 'Affiliate', the scope of responsibility in the contradicted text becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, as the term 'Affiliate' is no longer clearly defined within the contract, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means all financial, business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party solely in connection with the Evaluation, except that such term will not include (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality, (ii) information that is or becomes publicly known other than through a breach by the Receiving Party of any of its obligations under this Agreement, (iii) information received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not known by the Receiving Party to be under an obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party, (iv) information independently developed by the Receiving Party without reliance upon the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and (v) information that the Disclosing Party has disclosed to other parties without similar obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means all business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party solely in connection with the Evaluation, except that such term will not include (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality, (ii) information that is or becomes publicly known other than through a breach by the Receiving Party of any of its obligations under this Agreement, (iii) information received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not known by the Receiving Party to be under an obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party, (iv) information independently developed by the Receiving Party without reliance upon the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and (v) information that the Disclosing Party has disclosed to other parties without similar obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'financial' information from the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a contradiction because Section 2 states 'The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of...'. The evaluation involves financial considerations, making it unclear if financial information shared is actually covered by the NDA anymore. This creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and for no other purpose. The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is and will remain the property and asset of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'financial' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts the purpose outlined in the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the information is used for 'Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of...'. Since evaluation inherently involves financial information, excluding financial information from the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a strong contradiction because the evaluation itself would involve the use of information no longer protected. This would cause issues for enforcing the original intent of the contract because it causes ambiguity and uncertainty with the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used for Partner’s submission of its Application, and for no other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit purpose of 'evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace' creates a contradiction. Section 3 refers to the 'purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above', but the stated purpose is now vague, making it unclear what activities are actually permitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a purpose for the Confidential Information related to ADP Marketplace. The changed text removes this specific purpose, making the purpose vague. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to 'the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above'. Since the purpose is no longer clearly stated in Section 2, it creates ambiguity and a direct conflict with the reference in the 'Contradicted Text', as outlined in the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using reasonable care and discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion' creates a contradiction. While the first part mandates a certain level of care, omitting the defined minimum standards leaves the standard vague and unenforceable. This affects Section 7 which discusses unauthorized use, as it's now harder to prove a breach if the 'reasonable care' wasn't clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the Receiving Party should use 'no less than reasonable care and discretion', establishing a minimum standard. The changed text removes this minimum standard, making 'reasonable care and discretion' open to interpretation. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' because the ability to prove 'unauthorized use' or a 'breach' is now weakened, as there is no clear benchmark for what constitutes reasonable care. It adheres to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', where essential language is removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated elsewhere. Because Section 7 relies on a clear standard of care, removing the 'no less than' clause creates a logical inconsistency making it harder to enforce Section 7's requirements regarding unauthorized disclosure because determining 'reasonable care' is now ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information and any reproductions thereof. The authorized officer of the Receiving Party that is responsible for such return or destruction shall deliver a written certification to the Disclosing Party of such return or destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information and any reproductions thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause requiring 'The authorized officer of the Receiving Party that is responsible for such return or destruction shall deliver a written certification to the Disclosing Party of such return or destruction' creates an in-text contradiction. By removing this part it is going to be hard to prove that the receiving party, returned or destroyed any confidential information, since there is no certification being provided as a proof.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information and any reproductions thereof. The Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 2 and 3 will survive any return or destruction of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a requirement for the Receiving Party to provide a written certification upon returning or destroying Confidential Information. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text references the Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 2 and 3 surviving any return or destruction of Confidential Information. Without the written certification, it becomes difficult to prove that the Receiving Party actually returned or destroyed the Confidential Information, weakening the enforceability of Sections 2 and 3, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Omission of the clause requiring certification directly impacts the ability to verify compliance, which is crucial for the surviving obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees not to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party or refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees not to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'or refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party' creates an omission contradiction with the initial purpose of the NDA and Section 5. The document is about a potential partnership for marketing ADP services. By removing the restriction on referrals in promotional material, the receiving party could potentially use confidential information gained during evaluation (as marketing material) without written consent. This directly contradicts the confidentiality intentions of the original document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except for the obligation of confidentiality and the restrictions on use imposed by this Agreement upon the Receiving Party, each party acknowledges that no obligation of any kind is assumed or implied against the other party by virtue of any meeting or any discussion regarding the Evaluation with respect to whatever information is exchanged. Further, this Agreement and any meetings or communications of the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement will not (i) constitute any offer, request, or contract among the parties to engage in any transaction, nor (ii) constitute any offer, request or contract involving a buyer-seller relationship, venture, teaming or partnership relationship among the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented using the other party's trade name, service mark, or trademark, OR referring to the other party in promotional material without consent. The changed text removes the restriction on referrals in promotional material. The contradicted text states that 'Except for the obligation of confidentiality and the restrictions on use imposed by this Agreement upon the Receiving Party, each party acknowledges that no obligation of any kind is assumed'. By removing the restriction on referrals, the Receiving Party could potentially use confidential information gained during evaluation (as marketing material) without written consent. This contradicts the confidentiality intentions outlined in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the receiving party could potentially use confidential information gained during evaluation (as marketing material) without written consent.
